revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:京官]][[ko:경관직]][[zh:京官]]
'''Kyōkan''' (京官) refers to the offices and officials located in the capital under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. Also known as {{ill|Zaikyōshoshi|ja|在京諸司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaikyōshoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("various offices in the capital") or {{ill|Naikan|ja|内官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikan|qq=}}. Kyōkan contrasted with {{ill|Gaikan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}, the external offices. == Origins of Kyōkan == The concept of Kyōkan originates from the {{ill|Rites of Zhou|en|Rites of Zhou|qid=Q1367984|lt=Zhouli|1=Rites of Zhou}}, an ancient Chinese text, where officials under the direct control of the ruler managed the affairs of the capital and royal domain (王畿) while serving as the ruler’s aides. With the transition from the {{ill|Feudalism|en|Feudalism|qid=Q37739|lt=feudal system|1=Feudalism}} to the Ritsuryō system, regional lords (諸侯) were transformed into external offices, while the role of Kyōkan remained largely unchanged when adopted in Japan. For instance, the {{ill|Kushikiryō|ja|公式令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikiryō|1=Kushikiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392034}} prescribed different seals for official documents: the {{ill|Outer Seal|ja|外印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Seal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官印|1=Outer Seal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17225808}} (used by the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}) for Kyōkan and the {{ill|Inner Seal|ja|内印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Seal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御璽|1=Privy Seal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2915131}} (used by the Emperor) for Gaikan, a practice inherited from ancient China. === Introduction into Japan === In Japan, certain rules originally applicable to both Kyōkan and Gaikan in China were limited to Kyōkan, or entirely new rules were established. For example, high-Ranking Kyōkan officials of the fifth Rank or higher required Imperial approval to travel beyond the capital’s immediate region. Under the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|qid=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}, Kyōkan included the {{ill|Two Departments|ja|二官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two Departments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>The {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} and {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}</ref>, {{ill|Eight Ministries|ja|八省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ministries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193883}}<ref>The {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|qid=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center|1=Ministry of the Center}}, {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|qid=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|1=Shikibu-shō}}, {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|qid=Q10829855|lt=Ministry of Ceremonies|1=Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)}}, {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs|en|Ministry of Popular Affairs|qid=Q4388365|lt=Ministry of Popular Affairs|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs}}, {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|qid=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}, {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|qid=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}, {{ill|Ōkurashō|ja|大蔵省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurashō|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q910073}}, and {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Kunaishō|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}</ref>, {{ill|One Bureau|ja|一台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/One Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>The {{ill|Danjonokami|ja|弾正台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjonokami|1=Danjōdai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487784}}</ref>, and {{ill|Five Guards|ja|五衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Guards|1=Goefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372667}}<ref>The {{ill|Eimonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eimonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626338}} (left and right), {{ill|Eshifu|ja|衛士府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eshifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近衛兵|1=Royal guard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q971045}} (left and right), and {{ill|Hyōefu|ja|兵衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōefu|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393702}} (left and right).</ref>. Additional offices included the {{ill|Baryo|ja|馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryo|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}} (left and right), {{ill|Hyōgo|ja|兵庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo|1=Hyōgo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1177923}} (left, right, and internal), {{ill|Kyōshoku|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōshoku|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}} (left and right), and {{ill|Ichishi|ja|市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11480073}} (east and west). According to the code, these offices comprised 432 high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}}), and a total of 8,299 personnel when including lower-Ranking staff (such as {{ill|Shisei|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisei|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412126}}).<ref>This figure is based on the structure established under the Yōrō Code and does not account for later modifications, such as the transition of the Five Guards to Six Guards (e.g., the merging of the {{ill|Eimonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eimonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626338}} and {{ill|Eshifu|ja|衛士府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eshifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近衛兵|1=Royal guard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q971045}} into the {{ill|Eimonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eimonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626338}} and the creation of new guards like the {{ill|Satōkeifu|ja|授刀衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satōkeifu|1=Satōkeifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498159}} and {{ill|Chūkeifu|ja|中衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūkeifu|1=Chūkeifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109357391}}). Offices like the {{ill|Naikusashi|ja|内薬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikusashi|1=court physician|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394428}} and {{ill|San'iryō|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'iryō|1=Sanni Ryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499496}} were also abolished or merged.</ref> Offices outlined in the {{ill|Gogūshokuinryō|ja|後宮職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogūshokuinryō|1=Gogūshokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109357833}}, {{ill|Tōgūshokuinryō|ja|東宮職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgūshokuinryō|1=Tōgūshokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97215639}}, and {{ill|Karei Shokuinryō|ja|家令職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karei Shokuinryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were also considered part of Kyōkan. Additionally, the {{ill|Settsu Province Administration|ja|摂津職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province Administration|1=Settsu-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65269215}} was treated similarly, as noted in the {{ill|Ryōshūge|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshūge|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378321}}. According to the official rules, Kyōkan personnel reported to work before sunrise at the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} and {{ill|Chōdōin|ja|朝堂院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōdōin|1=Chōdō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11517360}}, and departed after the gates closed at noon. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Shigeru Tokinoya |title=Kyōkan |work=Kokushi Daijiten Volume 4 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |year=1984 |isbn=978-4-642-00504-3|qq=}} * {{Cite book|author=Kiyoyo Ōsumi |title=Kyōkan |work=Nihonshi Daijiten Volume 2 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993 |isbn=978-4-582-13102-4|qq=}} * {{Cite book|author=Tatsuya Torao |title=Kyōkan |work=Nihon Rekishi Daijiten Volume 1 |publisher=Shōgakukan |year=2000 |isbn=978-4-095-23001-6|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|京官|version=100426648|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:京官]]
<!--Central officials--><!--Central officials-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:경관직]] [[zh:京官]]
{{wikidata link|Q10883145}} [[Category:Officials of the Ritsuryō System]]
